summary:
The foramen caecum is accurately located in the frontal bone, specifically at its junction with the ethmoid bone, where it relates to nasociliary vein drainage. It is not anatomically recognized on the tongue or other skull bones such as the ethmoid or sphenoid.  

(Note: This summary focuses exclusively on clinically validated anatomical facts.)
